creator: khera@kcilink.com
text:
>>>>> "PP" == Peter Pentchev <roam@ringlet.net> writes:

>> Upon boot, the sound device, ESS Solo-1 is still recognized.  However, when
>> I try to use it, it either hangs or makes bad sounds.  When KDE fires up, it
>> just makes a loud static noise.  XEmacs tried to emit a sound and is now
>> wedged in "pcmsyn" state according to top.

PP> Could you look at the patch in PR kern/30794 and see if it helps?

I tried it last thursday (with some line fudge since there are changes
to the code since the PR), and it did not work.  That PR seems to
relate to resume after suspend, but I'm on a desktop, not a laptop.

I still have no luck with sound.
